title: Sarga 30
summary: Rama's divine birth, childhood, protection of Sage Vishwamitra's rituals from demons, breaking of Shiva's bow, and marriage to Sita. Addresses youth, childhood, education, overcoming early obstacles, and marriage.

sections.summary: This passage begins with “When the exceedingly powerful Mahendra was indeed conquered by Ravana's son, the gods then went to Lanka, making Prajapati their leader.” and develops toward “Having heard the imperishable words spoken by his grandfather, Lord Brahma, Indrajit (Meghanada then responded).”

This passage begins with “Then the mighty Meghanada spoke to Brahma, who was present there, saying, "Let it be heard, or may there be success in releasing Indra."” and develops toward “Then, I created a woman named Ahalya, endowed with qualities of beauty, for "Hala" here means ugliness or deformity, and "Halya" is that which arises…”

This passage begins with “Because no ugliness exists in her, she is renowned as Ahalya, and O Shakra, I thus established her name as Ahalya.” and develops toward “The sage then told you that whoever becomes the king of gods will not remain permanent, as this curse has been uttered by him.”

This passage begins with “Having rebuked his wife, the great ascetic then said, "O ill-behaved one, disappear from the vicinity of my hermitage!"” and develops toward “You must quickly perform a Vaishnava sacrifice with great concentration, and purified by that sacrifice, you will then ascend to heaven.”

This passage begins with “O king of gods, your son did not perish in the great battle; he was taken and kept safe by Aryaka in the great ocean.” and develops toward “O Rama, thus was born Ravana, a tormentor of the world, by whom, along with his son, Shakra, the lord of gods, was conquered in battle. इत्यार्षे…”

verses.sanskrit: जिते महेन्द्रे ऽतिबले रावणस्य सुतेन वै । प्रजापतिं पुरस्कृत्य ययुर्लङ्कां सुरास्तदा ।। 7.30.1 ।।
verses.translation: When the exceedingly powerful Mahendra was indeed conquered by Ravana's son, the gods then went to Lanka, making Prajapati their leader.

verses.sanskrit: तत्र रावणमासाद्य पुत्रभ्रातृभिरावृतम् । अब्रवीद्गगने तिष्ठन्सामपूर्वं प्रजापतिः ।। 7.30.2 ।।
verses.translation: There, Prajapati, standing in the sky, approached Ravana who was surrounded by his sons and brothers, and spoke to him first with gentle words.

verses.sanskrit: वत्स रावण तुष्टो ऽस्मि पुत्रस्य तव संयुगे । अहो ऽस्य विक्रमौदार्यं तव तुल्यो ऽधिको ऽपि वा ।। 7.30.3 ।।
verses.translation: Prajapati said, "Dear son Ravana, I am pleased with your son's performance in battle; oh, his valor and magnanimity are equal to yours, or perhaps even superior!"

verses.sanskrit: जितं हि भवता सर्वं त्रैलोक्यं स्वेन तेजसा । कृता प्रतिज्ञा सफला प्रीतो ऽस्मि स्वसुतेन वै ।। 7.30.4 ।।
verses.translation: Indeed, all three worlds have been conquered by your own prowess, and your vow has been made fruitful; I am truly pleased by your own son.

verses.sanskrit: अयं च पुत्रो ऽतिबलस्तव रावण वीर्यवान् । जगतीन्द्रजिदित्येव परिख्यातो भविष्यति ।। 7.30.5 ।।
verses.translation: This exceedingly strong and valorous son of yours, O Ravana, will indeed be renowned in the world as Indrajit.

verses.sanskrit: बलवान्दुर्जयश्चैव भविष्यत्येव राक्षसः । यं समाश्रित्य ते राजन्स्थापितास्त्रिदशा वशे ।। 7.30.6 ।।
verses.translation: This demon will indeed be powerful and unconquerable, O King, and it is by relying on him that you have kept the gods under your control.

verses.sanskrit: तन्मुच्यतां महाबाहो महेन्द्रः पाकशासनः । किञ्चास्य मोक्षणार्थाय प्रयच्छन्तु दिवौकसः ।। 7.30.7 ।।
verses.translation: Therefore, O mighty-armed one, let the great Indra, the slayer of Paka, be released, and let the gods also offer something for his liberation.

verses.sanskrit: अथाब्रवीन्महातेजा इन्द्रजित्समितिञ्जयः । अमरत्वमहं देव वृणे यद्येष मुच्यते ।। 7.30.8 ।।
verses.translation: Then, the greatly effulgent Indrajit, the conqueror in battle, spoke, saying, "O god, I choose immortality if this one (Indra) is released."

verses.sanskrit: चतुष्पदां खेचराणामन्येषां वा महौजसाम् । वृक्षगुल्मक्षुपलतातृणोपलमहीभृताम् ।। 7.30.9 ।।
verses.translation: This refers to various categories of beings and objects, including quadrupeds, sky-dwellers, other greatly powerful entities, and also trees, bushes, shrubs, creepers, grasses, stones, and mountains.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वे ऽपि जन्तवो ऽन्योन्यं भेतव्ये सति बिभ्यति । अतो ऽत्र लोके सर्वेषां सर्वस्माच्च भवेद्भयम् ।। 7.30.10 ।।
verses.translation: All living beings fear each other when there is cause for fear, and thus in this world, everyone may experience fear from everything.

verses.sanskrit: ततो ऽब्रवीन्महातेजा मेघनादं प्रजापतिः । नास्ति सर्वामरत्वं हि कस्यचित्प्राणिनो भुवि । चतुष्पदः पक्षिणश्च भूतानां वा महौजसाम् ।। 7.30.11 ।।
verses.translation: Then the greatly effulgent Prajapati Brahma spoke to Meghanada, stating that complete immortality indeed does not exist for any living being on earth, be it four-footed animals, birds, or even beings of great power.

verses.sanskrit: श्रुत्वा पितामहेनोक्तमिन्द्रजित्प्रभुणाव्ययम् ।। 7.30.12 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard the imperishable words spoken by his grandfather, Lord Brahma, Indrajit (Meghanada then responded).

verses.sanskrit: ्वं हि कस्यचित्प्राणिनो भुवि । अथाब्रवीत्स तत्रस्थं मेघनादो महाबलः । श्रूयतां वा भवेत्सिद्धिः शतक्रतुविमोक्षणे ।। 7.30.13 ।।
verses.translation: Then the mighty Meghanada spoke to Brahma, who was present there, saying, "Let it be heard, or may there be success in releasing Indra."

verses.sanskrit: ममेष्टं नित्यशो हर्व्यैर्मन्त्रैः सम्पूज्य पावकम् । सङ्ग्राममवतर्त्तुं च शत्रुनिर्जयकाङ्क्षिणः ।। 7.30.14 ।।
verses.translation: My desire is to always worship the fire with oblations and mantras, and then to enter battle, desiring victory over my enemies.

verses.sanskrit: अश्वयुक्तो रथो मह्यमुत्तिष्ठेत्तु विभावसोः । तत्स्थस्यामरता स्यान्मे एष मे निश्चयो वरः ।। 7.30.15 ।।
verses.translation: My best resolve is that a horse-yoked chariot of the sun should rise for me, and immortality should be granted to one standing on it.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मिन्यद्यसमाप्ते च जप्यहोमे विभावसौ । युध्येयं देव सङ्ग्रामे तदा मे स्याद्विनाशनम् ।। 7.30.16 ।।
verses.translation: O god, should I ever go to fight in battle while my fire-oblation rite remains unfinished, then in that case my destruction would surely follow.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वो हि तपसा देव वृणोत्यमरतां पुमान् । विक्रमेण मया त्वेतदमरत्वं प्रवर्तितम् ।। 7.30.17 ।।
verses.translation: O god, while every man indeed obtains immortality through penance, I have established this immortality through my valor.

verses.sanskrit: एवमस्त्विति तं चाह वाक्यं देवः पितामहः । मुक्तश्चेन्द्रजिता शक्रो गताश्च त्रिदिवं सुराः ।। 7.30.18 ।।
verses.translation: And thus, the god Brahma said to him, 'So be it,' whereupon Indra was released by Indrajit, and the gods returned to heaven.

verses.sanskrit: एतस्मिन्नन्तरे राम दीनो भ्रष्टाम्बरद्युतिः । इन्द्रश्चिन्तापरीतात्मा ध्यानतत्परतां गतः ।। 7.30.19 ।।
verses.translation: O Rama, in the meantime, Indra, distressed and having lost his celestial radiance, became absorbed in thought, his mind overwhelmed by worry.

verses.sanskrit: तं तु दृष्ट्वा तथाभूतं प्राह देवः प्रजापतिः । शतक्रतो किमु पुरा करोति स्म सुदुष्कृतम् ।। 7.30.20 ।।
verses.translation: Indeed, having seen him in such a state, the god Prajapati (Brahma) asked, 'O Shatakratu, what great evil deed did you commit formerly?'

verses.sanskrit: अमरेन्द्र मया बह्व्यः प्रजाः सृष्टास्तथा प्रभो । एकवर्णाः समाभाषा एकरूपाश्च सर्वशः ।। 7.30.21 ।।
verses.translation: O Lord Indra, I created many beings who were all of the same color, spoke the same language, and had the same form in every respect.

verses.sanskrit: तासां नास्ति विशेषो हि दर्शने लक्षणे ऽपि वा । ततो ऽहमेकाग्रमनास्ताः प्रजाः परिचिन्तयम् ।। 7.30.22 ।।
verses.translation: Indeed, there was no distinction among them in appearance or even in characteristics, so I pondered deeply about those creatures with a concentrated mind.

verses.sanskrit: सो ऽहं तासां विशेषार्थं स्त्रियमेकां विनिर्ममे । यद्यत्प्रजानां प्रत्यङ्गं विशिष्टं तत्तदुद्धृतम् ।। 7.30.23 ।।
verses.translation: Therefore, I created one woman for the sake of distinction among them, extracting whatever was excellent from each limb of the creatures.

verses.sanskrit: ततो मया रूपगुणैरहल्या स्त्री विनिर्मिता । हलं नामेह वैरूपं हल्यं तत्प्रभवं भवेत् ।। 7.30.24 ।।
verses.translation: Then, I created a woman named Ahalya, endowed with qualities of beauty, for "Hala" here means ugliness or deformity, and "Halya" is that which arises from it.

verses.sanskrit: यस्मान्न विद्यते हल्यं तेनाहल्येति विश्रुता । अहल्येति मया शक्र तस्या नाम प्रवर्तितम् ।। 7.30.25 ।।
verses.translation: Because no ugliness exists in her, she is renowned as Ahalya, and O Shakra, I thus established her name as Ahalya.

verses.sanskrit: निर्मितायां च देवेन्द्र तस्यां नार्यां सुरर्षभ । भविष्यतीति कस्यैषा मम चिन्ता ततो ऽभवत् ।। 7.30.26 ।।
verses.translation: O Indra, O best of gods, when that woman was created, a worry arose in me, thinking, "Whose will she be?"

verses.sanskrit: त्वं तु शक्र तदा नारीं जानीषे मनसा प्रभो । स्थानाधिकतया पत्नी ममैषेति पुरन्दर ।। 7.30.27 ।।
verses.translation: O Shakra, O Lord, O Purandara, you then mentally considered that woman to be your wife due to your superior position.

verses.sanskrit: सा मया न्यासभूता तु गौतमस्य महात्मनः । न्यस्ता बहूनि वर्षाणि तेन निर्यातिता च ह ।। 7.30.28 ।।
verses.translation: But she was entrusted by me as a deposit to the great-souled Gautama for many years, and indeed, she was returned by him.

verses.sanskrit: ततस्तस्य परिज्ञाय महास्थैर्यं महामुनेः । ज्ञात्वा तपसि सिद्धिं च पत्न्यर्थं स्पर्शिता तदा ।। 7.30.29 ।।
verses.translation: Then, having understood the great steadfastness of that great sage and his success in penance, she was offered to him as a wife.

verses.sanskrit: सङ्क्रुद्धस्त्वं हि धर्मात्मन् गत्वा तस्याश्रमं मुनेः । दृष्टवांश्च तदा तां स्त्रीं दीप्तामग्निशिखामिव ।। 7.30.30 ।।
verses.translation: Indeed, you, being very angry, O righteous one, went to that sage's hermitage and then saw that woman shining like a flame of fire.

verses.sanskrit: सा त्वया धर्षिता शक्र कामार्तेन समन्युना । दृष्टस्त्वं च तदा तेन ह्याश्रमे परमर्षिणा ।। 7.30.31 ।।
verses.translation: O Shakra, she was violated by you, who were afflicted by lust and anger, and you were then indeed seen in the hermitage by the great sage.

verses.sanskrit: ततः क्रुद्धेन तेना ऽसि शप्तः परमतेजसा । गतो ऽसि येन देवेन्द्र दशाभागविपर्ययम् ।। 7.30.32 ।।
verses.translation: Then, by that enraged sage of great ascetic power, you were cursed, O king of gods, by which you have undergone a reversal of your state.

verses.sanskrit: यस्मान्मे धर्षिता पत्नी त्वया वासव निर्भयम् । तस्मात्त्वं समरे राजञ्छत्रुहस्तं गमिष्यसि ।। 7.30.33 ।।
verses.translation: Because my wife was fearlessly violated by you, O Vasava, therefore, O king, you will fall into the hands of your enemies in battle.

verses.sanskrit: अयं तु भावो दुर्बुद्धे यस्त्वयेह प्रवर्तितः । मानुषेष्वपि लोकेषु भविष्यति न संशयः ।। 7.30.34 ।।
verses.translation: But this consequence, O evil-minded one, which you have initiated here, will undoubtedly also manifest in the human worlds.

verses.sanskrit: तत्रार्धं तस्य यः कर्ता त्वय्यर्धं निपतिष्यति । न च ते स्थावरं स्थानं भविष्यति न संशयः ।। 7.30.35 ।।
verses.translation: In that matter, half of the consequence will fall upon the doer, and half upon you, and undoubtedly, you will not have a fixed abode.

verses.sanskrit: यश्च यश्च सुरेन्द्रः स्याद्ध्रुवः स न भविष्यति । एष शापो मया मुक्त इत्यसौ त्वां तदा ऽब्रवीत् ।। 7.30.36 ।।
verses.translation: The sage then told you that whoever becomes the king of gods will not remain permanent, as this curse has been uttered by him.

verses.sanskrit: तां तु भार्यां स निर्भर्त्स्य सो ऽब्रवीत्सुमहातपाः । दुर्विनीते विनिध्वंस ममाश्रमसमीपतः ।। 7.30.37 ।।
verses.translation: Having rebuked his wife, the great ascetic then said, "O ill-behaved one, disappear from the vicinity of my hermitage!"

verses.sanskrit: रूपयौवनसम्पन्ना यस्मात्त्वमनवस्थिता । तस्माद्रूपवती लोके न त्वमेका भविष्यति ।। 7.30.38 ।।
verses.translation: Because you, endowed with beauty and youth, are unstable, therefore you will not be the only beautiful woman in the world.

verses.sanskrit: रूपं च ते प्रजाः सर्वा गमिष्यन्ति न संशयः । यत्तदेकं समाश्रित्य विभ्रमो ऽयमुपस्थितः ।। 7.30.39 ।।
verses.translation: All creatures will undoubtedly obtain your beauty, having relied upon which unique beauty this delusion has arisen.

verses.sanskrit: तदाप्रभृति भूयिष्ठं प्रजा रूपसमन्विताः । सा तं प्रसादयामास महर्षिं गौतमं तदा ।। 7.30.40 ।।
verses.translation: From that time onwards, most creatures became endowed with beauty, and then she (Ahalya) propitiated the great sage Gautama.

verses.sanskrit: अज्ञानाद्धर्षिता विप्र त्वद्रूपेण दिवौकसा । न कामकाराद्विप्रर्षे प्रसादं कर्तुमर्हसि ।। 7.30.41 ।।
verses.translation: O Brahmana-sage, I was deceived by the celestial being in your guise due to ignorance, not willingly, so you ought to grant me your grace.

verses.sanskrit: अहल्यया त्वेवमुक्तः प्रत्युवाच स गौतमः । उत्पत्स्यति महातेजा इक्ष्वाकूणां महारथः ।। 7.30.42 ।।
verses.translation: Thus addressed by Ahalya, Gautama replied that a greatly effulgent and great warrior of the Ikshvaku dynasty would be born.

verses.sanskrit: रामो नाम श्रुतो लोके वनं चाप्युपयास्यति । ब्राह्मणार्थे महाबाहुर्विष्णुर्मानुषविग्रहः । तं द्रक्ष्यसि यदा भद्रे ततः पूता भविष्यसि ।। 7.30.43 ।।
verses.translation: He will be known as Rama in the world, will come to the forest for the sake of Brahmanas, being the mighty-armed Vishnu in human form; when you see him, O auspicious one, you will then become purified.

verses.sanskrit: स हि पावयितुं शक्तस्त्वया यद्दुष्कृतं कृतम् । तस्यातिथ्यं च कृत्वा वै मत्समीपं गमिष्यसि ।। 7.30.44 ।।
verses.translation: He is indeed capable of purifying whatever evil deed you have committed, and after offering him hospitality, you will return to my side.

verses.sanskrit: वत्स्यसि त्वं मया सार्धं तदा हि वरवर्णिनि । एवमुक्त्वा स विप्रर्षिराजगाम स्वमाश्रमम् ।। 7.30.45 ।।
verses.translation: Then, O beautiful lady, you will indeed dwell with me; having spoken thus, the Brahmana-sage returned to his own hermitage.

verses.sanskrit: तपश्चाचार सुमहत्सा पत्नी ब्रह्मवादिनः । शापोत्सर्गाद्धि तस्येदं मुनेः सर्वमुपस्थितम् ।। 7.30.46 ।।
verses.translation: Indeed, that wife of the knower of Brahman performed very great penance, and all this has come to pass from the utterance of the sage's curse.

verses.sanskrit: तत्स्मर त्वं महाबाहो दुष्कृतं यत्त्वया कृतम् । तेन त्वं ग्रहणं शत्रोर्यातो नान्येन वासव ।। 7.30.47 ।।
verses.translation: O mighty-armed Vasava, remember that evil deed which you committed; by that alone have you been captured by the enemy, and not by any other means.

verses.sanskrit: शीघ्रं वै यज यज्ञं त्वं वैष्णवं सुसमाहितः । पावितस्तेन यज्ञेन यास्यसे त्रिदिवं ततः ।। 7.30.48 ।।
verses.translation: You must quickly perform a Vaishnava sacrifice with great concentration, and purified by that sacrifice, you will then ascend to heaven.

verses.sanskrit: पुत्रश्च तव देवेन्द्र न विनष्टो महारणे । नीतः सन्निहितश्चैव आर्यकेण महोदधौ ।। 7.30.49 ।।
verses.translation: O king of gods, your son did not perish in the great battle; he was taken and kept safe by Aryaka in the great ocean.

verses.sanskrit: एतच्छ्रुत्वा महेन्द्रस्तु यज्ञमिष्ट्वा च वैष्णवम् । पुनस्त्रिदिवमाक्रामदन्वशासच्च देवराट् ।। 7.30.50 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard this, Mahendra performed the Vaishnava sacrifice, and the king of gods then ascended to heaven again and ruled.

verses.sanskrit: एतदिन्द्रजितो नाम बलं यत्कीर्तितं मया । निर्जितस्तेन देवेन्द्रः प्राणिनो ऽन्ये तु किं पुनः ।। 7.30.51 ।।
verses.translation: This is the power of Indrajit that I have recounted, by which even the lord of gods, Indra, was conquered; what then to speak of other living beings?

verses.sanskrit: आश्चर्यमिति रामश्च लक्ष्मणश्चाब्रवीत्तदा । अगस्त्यवचनं श्रुत्वा वानरा राक्षसास्तदा ।। 7.30.52 ।।
verses.translation: Then, having heard the words of Agastya, Rama and Lakshmana, along with the monkeys and demons, exclaimed, "Wonderful!"

verses.sanskrit: विभीषणस्तु रामस्य पार्श्वस्थो वाक्यमब्रवीत् । आश्चर्यं स्मारितो ऽस्म्यद्य यत्तद्दृष्टं पुरातनम् ।। 7.30.53 ।।
verses.translation: But Vibhishana, standing by Rama's side, spoke, saying, "I am reminded today of that ancient wonder which I had seen."

verses.sanskrit: अगस्त्यं त्वब्रवीद्रामः सत्यमेतच्छ्रुतं च मे ।। 7.30.54 ।।
verses.translation: But Rama said to Agastya, "This is true, and I have also heard it."

verses.sanskrit: एवं राम समुद्भूतो रावणो लोककण्टकः । सपुत्रो येन सङ्ग्रामे जितः शक्रः सुरेश्वरः ।। 7.30.55 ।।
verses.translation: O Rama, thus was born Ravana, a tormentor of the world, by whom, along with his son, Shakra, the lord of gods, was conquered in battle. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये श्रीमदुत्तरकाण्डे त्रिंशः सर्गः ।। 30 ।। Thus ends the thirtieth sarga of Uttara Kanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
